summary: The bootstrap flow now clearly routes through a deep-interview-quality brief, the generated hook/template surfaces agree on the new push-gate policy, and the new pre-push template is backed by validator and smoke-test evidence. The only caveat is intentional: command slots for lint/typecheck/test/build/desloppify remain project-owned placeholders, so initialized repos still need one follow-through edit after bootstrap.

finding_log.summary: The generated `pre_push_check.sh` now has a real staged flow, but the lint/typecheck/test/build/desloppify command variables are intentionally left for the initialized repo to fill in after bootstrap.
evidence: The template runs real size-gate logic immediately and prints `Skip ... not configured` for unset project-owned commands.
The skill and README explicitly instruct the repo owner to fill those commands during bootstrap follow-through.
finding_log.next_action: No repair needed for this ticket; keep the follow-through step explicit in generated project setup.

rubric_sections.name: code-quality
findings: Responsibility is localized well: `deep-interview` owns intake quality, `init-project` owns scaffold defaults, and the shell template keeps the hook thin.
The new pre-push template is simple enough to audit and already handles the main failure path with a deterministic block plus remediation command.

rubric_sections.name: debloatability
findings: The change avoids cloning the deep-interview loop into `init-project` and instead makes the ownership split explicit, which reduces future drift risk.
Shared utility guidance is split cleanly between operational reminders and project-specific policy instead of duplicating the same rule everywhere.
rubric_sections.next_action: Good enough to pass; keep future bootstrap additions on the same ownership split.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The bootstrap script, templates, and skill docs now agree on visible brief ownership, manual hook activation, and optional CodeRabbit/desloppify behavior.
The only remaining dependency is the expected project-local command fill-in step after bootstrap, which is documented rather than hidden.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: The ticket claims are backed by three passing repo validators plus two temp-repo smoke tests showing warn-at-500 and block-at-1000 behavior.
The changed docs and templates were reviewed together, which reduces claim inflation about the ownership split or hook behavior.

next_action: Move the ticket to documenting-ready state with the attached review artifact and verification commands recorded in `Evidence`.
